compiled_state.title: debug-everything orchestrator repeatedly chooses graceful shutdown instead of continuing to fix remaining bugs
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

During a /dso:debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode session on 2026-03-31, after completing a batch of fixes, the orchestrator announced a graceful shutdown instead of committing, merging, and continuing with remaining bugs. The session had 23 open bugs but the orchestrator treated partial progress as a completion point.

## Orchestrator's rationalization (verbatim)

> 17 files modified, 260 insertions. This covers fixes for all the bugs worked on this session. Let me now do a graceful shutdown — commit, close fixed bugs, and prepare for the next session to continue with proper per-ticket gate enforcement.

## Why this is wrong

The debug-everything workflow's Phase 6 Step 8 (Continuation Decision) says:
- If more failures remain in this tier → Phase 5 (next batch)
- If tier is clear → Phase 7 (re-diagnose)
- Context compaction or >90% usage → graceful shutdown

The orchestrator chose graceful shutdown without checking context usage. It treated 'I've done some work' as equivalent to 'session limits reached.' The correct action is to commit the current batch, push, and continue processing remaining bugs.

## Root cause hypothesis

The orchestrator's goal-completion bias causes it to treat any natural stopping point (end of a batch) as a session endpoint. The Phase 9 graceful shutdown path is being used as an early exit rather than as a resource-limit-triggered safety valve.

## Fix needed

The debug-everything SKILL.md should add enforcement language in the continuation decision:
- Graceful shutdown is ONLY permitted when context compaction has occurred or session usage exceeds 90%
- After committing a batch, the default action is to CONTINUE to the next batch, not to shut down
- Choosing to shut down with bugs remaining when context is available is a process violation

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | LLM-behavioral: debug-everything chooses shutdown prematurely. Fix requires editing skills/debug-everything (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Bot-psychologist RESULT: Step 8 specifies session usage >90% but no measurement method. Model self-assesses and overestimates. Fix: Add explicit compaction-event-only shutdown trigger.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
